sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The white dot moved from [45,3, 36] to [42, 36] - it moved up by 3 pixels up. The blue cross parts also moved. This suggests the white dot is the player, and the blue cross is the player character, and when it moves, the blue cross follows.
Now I need to understand the game mechanics:
- White dot is the player (W) = white) is the player
- The blue cross moves with it (the blue cross parts)
- The yellow cross is stationary
- The gray squares with colored dots are scattered around
Let me think about what the goal. The game seems to be about moving the white dot (player) to collect or interact with something. The gray squares have colored centers (yellow or blue dots inside charcoal squares).
Looking at the layout:
- Yellow cross at [3, 15] at [9, 6] at [9, 24] at [17, 15]
- Blue dots at [16, 48], [24, 40], [24, 53], [35, 48]
- Player (white dot) at [45, 36]
- Yellow cross at ~row 16-38, col 21
- Blue cross at ~row 32-58, col 36
So the player is at the blue cross. The goal might be to navigate to collect the yellow dots or blue dots.
Let me think about what SPACE do something special. Let me try SPACE.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
seg = current_frame.segmentation
w_node = [n for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'W']
print("Player at:", w_node[0]['boundary'])
print("Player at:", w_node[0]['boundary'])
# Try SPACE to see what it does
action(['SPACE'])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
